channel.title: Query parameters for post bugging out on Vercel deployment
channel.description:
I have successfully deployed the website on Vercel and it has been amazing so far. However we are using something called **buffer.com** to manage social media posts.
**Problem:**
Buffer will create a link such as: https://whatsoninvers.nz/passionate-retailers-speak-out-about-cycle-lane/?fbclid=IwAR1-sKy23jvd-amqgSHIx2ecRwMH8w-zSiWKemI9KwpASUEPazRs5bbQU-M**&utm_campaign=buffer&utm_content=bufferf775a&utm_medium=social&utm_source=facebook.com**
It encounters the error: *Uncaught (in promise) InternalError: too much recursion*, and the page content and banner ads will not load, nor the recommended posts!
However if we trim the link down to: https://whatsoninvers.nz/passionate-retailers-speak-out-about-cycle-lane/ it will work (**even with the fbclickid parameter set**).
The strange thing is, if I try this link locally (npm run dev) it will work (with the original link)! Is this a Vercel issue? Has anyone encountered the same sort of issue?
Or in other words, **is there any way to completely sanitise these kinds of inputs?**

item.title: Query parameters for post bugging out on Vercel deployment
item.dc:creator: Pablo
item.description:
For future reference, this issue was solved in this other thread:

Problem with links with `utm` campaings 🙋Get Help
The PR has been merged and we just made a new release. Please update @frontity/core and @frontity/tiny-router to the latest version: Keep Frontity Updated - Frontity Docs

Read full topic
item.source: Query parameters for post bugging out on Vercel deployment

item.title: Query parameters for post bugging out on Vercel deployment
item.dc:creator: jamesjappy
item.description:
I have successfully deployed the website on Vercel and it has been amazing so far. However we are using something called buffer.com to manage social media posts.

Problem:

Buffer will create a link such as: Passionate Retailers Speak Out About Cycle Lane

It encounters the error: Uncaught (in promise) InternalError: too much recursion, and the page content and banner ads will not load, nor the recommended posts!

However if we trim the link down to: Passionate Retailers Speak Out About Cycle Lane it will work (even with the fbclickid parameter set).

The strange thing is, if I try this link locally (npm run dev) it will work (with the original link)! Is this a Vercel issue? Has anyone encountered the same sort of issue?

Or in other words, is there any way to completely sanitise these kinds of inputs?

Read full topic
item.source: Query parameters for post bugging out on Vercel deployment
